❯ /erk:objective-list ⎿ Here are all open objectives in the repository, formatted as a markdown table:
┌───────┬───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┬──────────────┐ │ # │ Title │ Created │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8762 │ Objective: Knowledge Promotion Pipeline │ Mar 5, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8760 │ Objective: Agent-DX for Erk Exec Commands │ Mar 5, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8655 │ Objective: Skill Architecture — Capture, Promote, Gate │ Mar 3, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8470 │ Objective: One-Shot Modification of Existing PRs │ Feb 28, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8384 │ Objective: Rewrite user-facing docs with composability-first │ Feb 27, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8381 │ Objective: Standardize Plan-as-PR Terminology Across Codebase │ Feb 27, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8149 │ Objective: Markdown-Driven Manual CLI Test Scenarios │ Feb 25, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8088 │ Objective: Standardized --json Output Across CLI │ Feb 24, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #8036 │ Objective: Ship Demo-Ready Agent-Mode erk-slack-bot │ Feb 24, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #7813 │ Objective: Eliminate Unnecessary Git Checkouts via Plumbing │ Feb 22, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #7160 │ Objective: Ensure-Based CLI Error Handling │ Feb 16, 2026 │ ├───────┼───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┼──────────────┤ │ #7159 │ Objective: Codex CLI Adoption Experience │ Feb 16, 2026 │ └───────┴───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┴──────────────┘
Summary
There are 12 open objectives, with the most recent created on March 5, 2026.
Suggested Next Steps
To work with these objectives:
- View details: /local:objective-view <number> — See dependency graph, sub-nodes, and progress - Create a plan: /erk:objective-plan <number> — Plan the next node in an objective - Check status: erk objective check <number> — Validate objective metadata
The newest objectives (#8762 and #8760) may be good candidates for planning if they don't yet have dependent work.
─────────────────────

## Summary
I've thoroughly explored the codebase and found the key components you need. Here's what I discovered:
---
### 1. The `/erk:objective-list` Skill
**Location:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.claude/commands/erk/objective-list.md`
This is a simple agent-driven skill that: - Fetches open objectives using `gh issue list --label "erk-objective" --state open --json number,title,createdAt --limit 50` - Displays results in a basic markdown table with 3 columns: `#`, `title`, `created` - Suggests follow-up commands (`/local:objective-view`, `/erk:objective-plan`)
**Note:** This CLI command shows minimal data and is NOT the source of the rich TUI data.
---
### 2. The `erk dash` TUI Objectives Tab - Rendering Code
**Location:** `/Users/schrockn/code/erk/src/erk/tui/widgets/plan_table.py`
The objectives view displays **9 columns** with the exact names you mentioned:
| Column | Key | Width | Source Field | |--------|-----|-------|--------------| | issue | plan | 6 | `plan_id` |

283→ # Objectives view: plan, slug, progress, state, deps-state, deps, next, updated, author 284→ if self._view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES: 285→ # Build linkified deps cell (show up to 3, truncate with ... if more) 286→ if row.objective_deps_plans: 287→ limit = 3 if len(row.objective_deps_plans) <= 3 else 2 288→ show = row.objective_deps_plans[:limit] 289→ parts = [f"[link={url}]{display}[/link]" for display, url in show] 290→ if len(row.objective_deps_plans) > 3: 291→ parts.append("\u2026") 292→ deps_cell = " ".join(parts) 293→ else: 294→ deps_cell = "-" 295→ 296→ return ( 297→ plan_cell, 298→ row.objective_slug_display, 299→ row.objective_progress_display, 300→ Text(row.objective_state_display), 301→ row.objective_deps_display, 302→ deps_cell, 303→ row.objective_next_node_display, 304→ row.updated_display, 305→ row.author, 306→ ) 307→ 308→ # Format worktree 309→ if row.exists_locally: 310→ wt_cell = row.worktree_name 311→ else: 312→ wt_cell = "-"

PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: DISPLAY PLAN: Before asking the question below, read the plan file and display its contents to the user with proper markdown formatting: /Users/schrockn/.claude/plans/squishy-brewing-dragonfly.md
PLAN SAVE PROMPT
A plan exists for this session but has not been saved.
Use AskUserQuestion to ask the user: question: "📋 Plan: Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard Current context: (wt:erk) (br:master) (plan:#8751)\n\nWhat would you like to do with this plan?" header: "br:master"
IMPORTANT: Present options in this exact order: 1. "Create new branch and planned PR. Stays on current branch." - Create a new branch, save plan as draft PR. 2. "Implement without saving" - Implement directly on the current branch without saving a plan PR. 3. "View/Edit the Plan" - Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.
⚠️ WARNING: Currently on 'master'. We strongly discourage implementing directly on the trunk branch. Consider saving the plan and implementing in a dedicated worktree instead.
If user chooses 'Create new branch and planned PR': 1. Run /erk:plan-save 2. Call ExitPlanMode to end the planning session.
If user chooses 'Implement without saving': 1. Create implement-now marker: erk exec marker create --session-id e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4 \ exit-plan-mode-hook.implement-now 2. Call ExitPlanMode 3. After exiting plan mode, implement the changes directly on the current branch. Do NOT run 'erk exec setup-impl' or create a new branch.

session.assistant:
Here's the plan:
## Plan: Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard
### Context
The `/erk:objective-list` Claude Code command shows a minimal 3-column table (issue, title, created) using raw `gh issue list`. The `erk dash` TUI objectives tab shows 9 rich columns (issue, slug, prog, state, deps-state, deps, next, updated, created by) by parsing roadmaps and computing dependency graphs. The goal is to make the CLI output match the TUI.
### Step 1: Enhance `erk objective list` CLI command
**File:** `src/erk/cli/commands/objective/list_cmd.py`
Add roadmap parsing and graph computation using the same functions as `RealPlanDataProvider._build_row_data()`. Update the Rich table to show 9 columns: issue, slug, prog, state, deps-state, deps, next, updated, created by.
### Step 2: Update the Claude Code skill
**File:** `.claude/commands/erk/objective-list.md`
Replace the `gh issue list` approach with simply running `erk objective list` and displaying output directly.
### Verification
1. Run `erk objective list` and compare to `erk dash` objectives tab 2. Run `/erk:objective-list` in Claude Code and verify rich data 3. Run fast-ci

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'What would you like to do with this plan?', 'header': 'br:master', 'options...[truncated 321 chars]...ion': 'Open plan in editor to review or modify before deciding.'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "What would you like to do with this plan?"="Create new branch and planned PR". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.user:
# /erk:plan-save
Save the current session's plan to GitHub with session context.
## Usage
```bash /erk:plan-save # Standalone plan /erk:plan-save --plan-type=learn # Learn plan (erk-learn label) /erk:plan-save --objective=123 # Explicit objective link (overrides marker) /erk:plan-save --current-branch # Use current branch instead of creating new plnd/ branch ```
Objective linking: if a plan was created via `/erk:objective-plan`, the session's objective-context marker is read automatically by the save command. Use `--objective=<number>` to override the marker or provide the link when no marker exists (e.g., during replan).
## Plan Storage
The plan is saved using the configured backend:
- **Draft PR backend** (`PLAN_BACKEND = "draft_pr"`): Creates a branch, pushes a plan commit, and opens a draft PR. Plan content is in the PR body after the metadata separator. The JSON output contract includes `plan_number`, `plan_url`, `title`, `branch_name`, `plan_backend`.
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Check `` for the `--plan-type`, `--objective`, and `--current-branch` flags:
``` If contains "--plan-type=<type>": - Extract the type (standard or learn) - Store as PLAN_TYPE variable - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to "--plan-type=<type>" Else: - Set PLAN_TYPE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--objective=<number>": - Extract the number - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to "--objective=<number>" Else: - Set OBJECTIVE_FLAG to empty string
If contains "--current-branch": - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to "--current-branch" Else: - Set CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG to empty string ```
### Step 2: Generate Branch Slug
**Skip this step if `--current-branch` was passed.** The current branch is used directly.
Before saving, generate a branch slug from the plan title. Read the plan title from the plan file you wrote in plan mode (the first `# ` heading).
Generate a branch slug from the title:
- 2-4 hyphenated lowercase words, max 30 characters - Capture distinctive essence, drop filler words (the, a, for, implementation, plan) - Prefer action verbs: add, fix, refactor, update, consolidate, extract, migrate - Examples: "fix-auth-session", "add-plan-validation", "refactor-gateway-abc"
Store the result as `BRANCH_SLUG`.
### Step 3: Generate Plan Summary
Write a concise 2-3 sentence summary of the plan. This summary will be visible at the top of the PR description (above the collapsed full plan).
Guidelines:
- Focus on WHAT the plan does and WHY - Do not repeat the title - Plain text, no markdown headers or formatting - Avoid special shell characters (backticks, dollar signs) - Store as PLAN_SUMMARY
### Step 4: Run Save Command
Run this command with the session ID, summary, and optional flags.
If CURRENT_BRANCH_FLAG is set, pass `--current-branch` (no branch slug needed):
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" --current-branch --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Otherwise, pass the branch slug:
```bash erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" --branch-slug="${BRANCH_SLUG}" --summary="${PLAN_SUMMARY}" ${PLAN_TYPE_FLAG} ${OBJECTIVE_FLAG} ```
Parse the JSON output to extract `plan_number` for verification in Step 5.
If the command fails, display the error and stop.
### Step 5: Verify Objective Link (if applicable)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` is non-null in the JSON output from Step 4.**
Verify the objective link was saved correctly:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <plan_number> objective_issue ```
Parse the JSON response:
- If `success: true` and `value` matches the expected objective number: verification passed - If `success: false` or value doesn't match: verification failed
**On verification success:**
Display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
**On verification failure:**
Display error and remediation steps:
``` ERROR: Objective link verification failed Expected objective: #<expected> Actual: <actual-or-null>
The plan was saved but without the correct objective link. Fix: Close draft PR #<plan_number>, ensure the objective-context marker exists, and re-run /erk:plan-save. ```
Exit without creating the plan-saved marker. The session continues so the user can retry.
### Step 6: Update Objective Roadmap (if objective linked)
**Only run this step if `objective_issue` was non-null in JSON output and verification passed.**
Update the objective's roadmap table to show that a plan has been created for this node:
1. **Read the roadmap node marker** to get the node ID:
```bash step_id=$(erk exec marker read --session-id "e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" roadmap-step) ```
If the marker doesn't exist (command fails), skip this step - the plan wasn't created via `objective-plan`.
2. **Update the roadmap table** using the dedicated command:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-issue> --node "$step_id" --pr "#<plan_number>" --status in_progress ```
This atomically fetches the objective body, finds the matching node row, sets the Status cell to `in-progress`, and writes the updated body back.
3. **Report the update:**
Display: `Updated objective #<objective-issue> roadmap: node <step_id> → plan #<plan_number>`
**Error handling:** If the roadmap update fails, warn but continue - the plan was saved successfully, just the roadmap tracking didn't update. The user can manually update the objective.
### Step 7: Display Results
**If JSON contains `skipped_duplicate: true`:**
Display: `Plan already saved as #<plan_number> (duplicate skipped)`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options.
**Otherwise, on success**, display:
``` Plan "<title>" saved as draft PR #<plan_number> URL: <issue_url> ```
If objective was verified, also display: `Verified objective link: #<objective-number>`
Then call ExitPlanMode. The exit-plan-mode hook will present "what next?" options (implement in current worktree, implement in new worktree, or done).
On failure, display the error message and suggest:
- Checking that a plan exists (enter Plan mode and exit it first) - Verifying GitHub CLI authentication (`gh auth status`) - Checking network connectivity
### Step 5: Push Planning Session
After saving successfully, push the planning session for cross-machine learning. This captures the in-progress session state so it's available even if implementation happens on a different machine.
Run `erk exec capture-session-info` to get the session file path, then push it:
```bash erk exec upload-impl-session --session-id "e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" 2>/dev/null || true ```
If the plan was saved successfully and `plan_number` is known, use push-session directly for better stage tracking:
```bash erk exec push-session \ --session-file "<session_file_path>" \ --session-id "e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" \ --stage planning \ --source local \ --plan-id <plan_number> \ 2>/dev/null || true ```
Where `<session_file_path>` is obtained from `erk exec capture-session-info` output and `<plan_number>` is from Step 2's JSON output.
**Note:** This is non-critical. If it fails, the plan was still saved successfully. The `2>/dev/null || true` ensures graceful degradation.
## Session Tracking
After successfully saving a plan, the plan number is stored in a marker file that enables automatic plan updates in the same session.
**To read the saved plan number:**
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" plan-saved-issue ```
This returns the plan number (exit code 0) or exits with code 1 if no plan was saved in this session.
session.assistant: Running the plan save command now.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: erk exec plan-save --format json --session-id="e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" --bra...[truncated 210 chars]...eated by). Also simplifies the Claude Code skill to delegate to the enriched CLI command."
Save plan as draft PR via erk exec plan-save

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "plan_number": 8777, "plan_url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8777", "title": "[erk-plan] Plan: Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard", "branch_name": "plnd/enrich-objective-list-cli-03-05-1107", "plan_backend": "planned_pr", "objective_issue": null, "archived_to": "/Users/schrockn/code/erk/.erk/scratch/sessions/e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4/plan_snapshots/000001-d888113d"}
session.assistant: Plan saved. Now pushing the planning session.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Push planning session for cross-machine learning

session.assistant: Plan **"Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard"** saved as draft PR **#8777**. URL: https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8777

tool_use.name: ExitPlanMode
tool_use.param:
# Plan: Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard
## Context
The `/erk:obje...[truncated 2347 chars]...laude Code and verify it shows the same rich data 3. Run fast-ci to ensure no regressions

session.tool_result:
PreToolUse:ExitPlanMode hook error: [command -v erk >/dev/null 2>&1 || exit 0; ERK_HOOK_ID=exit-plan-mode-hook erk exec exit-plan-mode-hook]: Plan #8777 saved successfully.
Display ALL of the following next-steps commands to the user as plain text. Show every line exactly as written — do NOT summarize, truncate, or omit any lines. (do NOT use AskUserQuestion — just display the text):
Implement plan #8777: In current wt: source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement -d In new wt: source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement (dangerously): source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement -d
Checkout plan #8777: In current wt: erk br co --for-plan 8777 In new wt: erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777
Dispatch plan #8777: CLI command: erk pr dispatch 8777 Slash command: /erk:pr-dispatch 8777
Session complete. Do NOT call ExitPlanMode again.
session.assistant:
Plan #8777 saved successfully.
**Next steps for plan #8777:**
Implement plan #8777: In current wt: `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement` (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement -d` In new wt: `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement` (dangerously): `source "$(erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777 --script)" && erk implement -d`
Checkout plan #8777: In current wt: `erk br co --for-plan 8777` In new wt: `erk br co --new-slot --for-plan 8777`
Dispatch plan #8777: CLI command: `erk pr dispatch 8777` Slash command: `/erk:pr-dispatch 8777`
session.user:
# /erk:pr-dispatch
## Goal
Find the most recent plan created in this conversation and dispatch it for remote AI implementation via `erk pr dispatch`.
## What This Command Does
1. Search conversation for the last plan reference 2. Extract the plan number 3. Run `erk pr dispatch <plan_number>` to trigger remote implementation
## Finding the Plan
Search the conversation from bottom to top for these patterns (in priority order):
1. **Draft PR reference**: `saved as draft PR #<number>` or `draft PR #<number>` 2. **Pull request URL**: `https://github.com/<owner>/<repo>/pull/<number>` 3. **Plan URL**: `https://github.com/.../pull/<number>` (legacy: `https://github.com/.../issues/<number>`)
Extract the plan number from the most recent match.
## Execution
Once you have the plan number, run:
```bash erk pr dispatch <plan_number> ```
If the command succeeds, clear the session marker to allow creating new plans in this session:
```bash erk exec marker delete --session-id "e3a363c5-ccae-4023-a86a-736b57a5afb4" plan-saved-issue ```
Display the command output to the user. The `erk pr dispatch` command handles all validation (plan existence, labels, state).
## Error Cases
- **No plan found in conversation**: Report "No plan found in conversation. Run /erk:plan-save first to create a plan." - **erk pr dispatch fails**: Display the error output from the command (erk pr dispatch validates the plan)
session.assistant: The most recent plan is **#8777**. Dispatching it for remote implementation.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Dispatch plan #8777 for remote AI implementation

session.tool_result:
Checking GitHub authentication... Syncing trunk with remote... Resolving GitHub username... Validating 1 planned-PR plan(s)...
Validating PR #8777...
✓ All 1 plan(s) validated
#8777: [erk-plan] Plan: Enrich `erk objective list` CLI to match TUI dashboard
Dispatching PR #8777...
Fetching plan content... Syncing branch: plnd/enrich-objective-list-cli-03-05-1107 Committing plan to branch... ✓ Branch pushed to remote
Dispatching workflow: plan-implement.yml Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 1/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 2/11) Waiting for workflow run... (attempt 3/11) ✓ Workflow dispatched. ✓ Dispatch metadata written Posting queued event comment... ✓ Queued event comment posted
✓ 1 plan(s) dispatched successfully!
... [5 lines omitted] ...

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Clear plan-saved marker to allow new plans

session.assistant:
Plan #8777 dispatched successfully for remote implementation.
- **Plan:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/pull/8777 - **Workflow:** https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/actions/runs/22732428489
session.user: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
